growthcleanr 2.99.0
  • Articles
  • Reference
  • Changelog

Run cleangrowth() with automatic checkpointing for large datasets

Source: R/cleangrowth_checkpoint.R
cleangrowth_checkpoint.Rd

Splits subjects into chunks, saves each chunk's result to a checkpoint file, and skips already-completed chunks on restart. When a long run is interrupted or fails, re-running the same call resumes from the last completed chunk rather than starting over.

cleangrowth_checkpoint(
  data,
  checkpoint_dir,
  chunk_size = 5000,
  log_file = NULL,
  keep_checkpoints = TRUE,
  use_existing_checkpoint_results = FALSE,
  quietly = FALSE,
  verbose = FALSE,
  ...
)

Arguments

data

data.table (or object coercible to one) with columns subjid, param, agedays, sex, measurement, and id. Column names must match exactly.

checkpoint_dir

Directory for per-chunk checkpoint files (.rds). Created if it does not exist. Use a dedicated directory per dataset/run to avoid mixing checkpoints from different runs.

chunk_size

Number of subjects per chunk. Default 5000. Aim for 20–60 minutes of runtime per chunk. The number of chunks is capped at 1000: if the default would exceed that, chunk_size is automatically increased to stay within the cap; an explicitly supplied chunk_size that would exceed it is an error. When resuming with use_existing_checkpoint_results = TRUE, use the same chunk_size (and dataset) as the original run.

log_file

Optional path to a log file. Messages are always written to the console; if provided, they are also appended to this file so a colleague can monitor progress remotely.

keep_checkpoints

Logical. Controls only what happens to the per-chunk checkpoint files after a successful run. If TRUE (default), they are left in checkpoint_dir; if FALSE, they are deleted once the combined result is returned. Independent of use_existing_checkpoint_results, which governs the start of the run.

use_existing_checkpoint_results

Logical. If FALSE (default), the dataset is cleaned fresh: any checkpoint files already in checkpoint_dir from a prior run are deleted at the start, so the combined result contains only chunks from this run. If TRUE, existing checkpoint files are reused – chunks that already have a checkpoint are skipped – to resume an interrupted run or re-combine prior results. When TRUE, you are responsible for using the same dataset and chunk_size as the run that wrote those files.

quietly

Logical (default FALSE). When FALSE, prints progress: a run header, a prominent banner per chunk (the most visible "where am I" marker), the per-chunk cleaning milestones, and per-chunk plus total elapsed time. When TRUE, the console is silent; if log_file is set it still receives the full progress log for remote monitoring.

verbose

Logical (default FALSE). When TRUE, additionally prints the granular per-step diagnostics from cleangrowth(). Implies quietly = FALSE.

...

Additional arguments passed to cleangrowth (e.g. cf_rescue, adult_permissiveness). ref_tables is handled internally - reference tables are pre-loaded once and reused across all chunks. Do not pass ref_tables here. recenter_source is forwarded to each chunk, so a chunked run recenters exactly like an unchunked one.

Value

A data.table with the same structure as cleangrowth output, with rows in the original input order.

Details

Checkpoint files are named chunk_0001.rds, chunk_0002.rds, etc. in checkpoint_dir. By default a run starts fresh, deleting any checkpoint files already present in checkpoint_dir. With use_existing_checkpoint_results = TRUE, any chunk that already has a checkpoint file is skipped, so an interrupted run resumes where it left off.

If the run completes but the combine step fails, use cleangrowth_checkpoint_combine to re-combine the saved checkpoint files without re-running the algorithm.

To check progress during a run, count the .rds files in checkpoint_dir or read the log file. The log file is safe to read while the run is in progress.

recenter_source ("reference" or "none") and any user-supplied sd.recenter table passed via ... are forwarded to every chunk unchanged, so a chunked run recenters identically to an unchunked one.

For a run redirected to a log file from the shell:

Rscript run_gc.R >> results/run_log.txt 2>&1 &

See also

cleangrowth_checkpoint_combine

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
library(data.table)
dt <- fread("large_dataset.csv")

result <- cleangrowth_checkpoint(
  data           = dt,
  checkpoint_dir = "results/gc_checkpoints",
  chunk_size     = 5000,
  log_file       = "results/gc_run.log"
)
} # }

Contents
